title: Disable inbox notifications unless assigned to item or mentioned

posts.author: sjregan (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml:
• Tell us your use case.

We have a team of developers, some who are constantly working on items on a board, others who need to see and interact with the board occasionally.

  • Frame the feature request/feedback in context with your pain point and what you want to achieve.

In order for someone to see a private board they have to be a member of the board. If you are a member of the board your inbox gets flooded with notifications regardless of whether or not the items relate to you. This creates a lot of noise and busywork, you have to constantly tick ‘read’ on items that have nothing to do with you or just ignore the inbox completely.

  • Help us understand the impact this is having on your team; is this having a critical impact that’s affecting your team’s productivity? Is this causing roadblocks in adopting as your main work tool?

Users are not seeing items relating to them because they are hard to see in the constant noise of unrelated notifications. Critical work is being delayed.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @sjregan

Welcome to the community. Did you play around with the settings in Avatar > profile > notifications? Also: are you referring to your email inbox or to the monday inbox?

posts.author: sjregan (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you. I am referring to the Monday inbox so the email notifications settings aren’t relevant in this case thanks.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree. My work with monday primarily is primarily behind the scenes and on the technical side.
In the work I have been doing lately I learned that almost none of our people use either the inbox or bell icon. They just see them as having WAY to much noise to be useful.

posts.author: keames (K.C. Eames)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, seeing the same issue with our team. No one uses it. Also, not clear on Inbox vs. Notifications, more customizability would be great. Inbox vs. Notifications

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, same issue for us. I would be nice if in the board setting will be an option that all subscribers of the board will be receiving notification from all item or the option of receiving notification just for items that each one is subscribed to.

posts.author: joiniart (Edward P)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this feature yet available? It is annoying for us that we are getting notifications on things we aren’t related to

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml:
It seems that this problems had been recently solved by monday.com… we are still testing but these are great news.

Screen Shot 2022-07-25 at 11.42.151041×1031 147 KB

New Feature: Mute Board Notifications New Features
Hi everyone! We wanted to tell the community first about our newest feature - Mute board notifications! You can now choose to completely mute notifications from specific boards or only get notifications where you or your team have been mentioned! How can you access this feature? click on your avatar icon on the bottom left and then go to monday.labs [image] Click on the three dots menu and then notification settings and choose your preference! [image] Please share you…

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml:
After testing this new feature it seems that it is possible to mute notification, but as for example automation create an update, the notification will still come to the inbox.

Probably it would be nice if the same feature could be apply to the inbox notification, so it can be given the option to turn off in the inbox as well.

posts.author: sergeybrocardkevan (Sergey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!

I can’t seem to find this in monday labs, and seeing that it’s 2 years ago, did yall manage to make it into a legitimate feature? Let me know! I really want this feature as I would like to pick and choose who gets notifications for the board, and who wouldn’t.
